name: Executive Data Storytelling
description: A board-ready data narrative system with navy headers, bright analytical accents, comparison cards, and evidence-led conclusions.
style_guide:
# Executive Data Storytelling

## Visual concept

This English-language style is designed for executive briefings, market comparisons, economic outlooks, investor updates, and policy analysis. It treats charts as part of a narrative rather than as isolated dashboard widgets. Every page should establish the question, show the evidence, and end with an implication or decision. The visual language combines a deep navy report header with clean light analysis panels, generous spacing, and a disciplined accent palette.

## Canvas, grid, and safe area

- Use a fixed `1280 x 720 px` canvas in a 16:9 ratio.
- Keep a `60–72 px` outer safe margin. Headers may use a full-width band, but all text, charts, and cards stay inside the content safe area.
- Use a 12-column grid with `20–24 px` gutters. Typical layouts are 6/6 comparison panels, 7/5 chart-plus-insight, 4/8 timeline-plus-chart, and 3-column evidence cards.
- Reserve a `96–116 px` header band for the section label, page title, source or forecast marker, and one compact icon or status block.
- Keep the body background light on analytical pages. End with a footer containing source text on the left and a quiet slide number on the right.
- Align card tops, chart frames, legends, and conclusion panels to shared grid edges. Avoid masonry layouts that make reading order ambiguous.

## Color system

### Core colors

- Report navy: `#1F2B3D`. Use for header bands, dark outlook panels, and high-priority conclusion cards.
- Ink: `#273246`. Use for headings, values, table labels, and primary body emphasis.
- Action blue: `#3F7FEA`. Use for the primary series, rules, active bars, header accents, and the first comparison entity.
- Signal teal: `#20B3A5`. Use for the second comparison entity, stable or mature states, and a secondary chart series.
- Insight violet: `#635BEA`. Use for structural highlights, critical labels, and a third category when needed.

### Supporting colors

- Alert coral: `#F0445D`. Use only for risk, decline, aging, or negative movement.
- Soft blue: `#EEF4FE`. Use for first-entity KPI cards, highlighted baselines, and light callout fills.
- Soft teal: `#EAF9F6`. Use for second-entity cards and positive or stable callouts.
- Soft violet: `#F0EFFE`. Use for structural conclusions and selected status pills.
- Paper: `#F7F9FC`. Use as the analytical page background.
- White: `#FFFFFF`. Use for cards, chart frames, and high-contrast content surfaces.
- Body gray: `#68758A`. Use for subtitles, descriptions, axis labels, and source notes.
- Grid gray: `#E3E8EF`. Use for borders, dividers, and chart grid lines.

### Usage rules

- Action blue is the default primary emphasis. Signal teal is the default comparison color. Keep Alert coral semantic and rare.
- Use a maximum of four saturated colors on one page, and always explain non-neutral series with a compact legend.
- Use Report navy for a header or one conclusion panel, not as a full-page background on every slide.
- Never use color as the only encoding for a claim. Pair color with labels, position, line style, or direct values.
- Do not introduce decorative gradients, metallic effects, or unrelated brand colors.

## Typography

- Primary font: `Inter` or `Arial`; fallback: `Helvetica Neue, system-ui, sans-serif`.
- Use bold, compact sans-serif display type for analytical titles and regular sans-serif for explanatory copy.
- Cover title: `54–76 px`, weight `800–900`, line-height `0.98–1.05`; keep the title to two lines or fewer.
- Page title: `36–48 px`, weight `800–900`, line-height `1.05–1.12`.
- Section label: `13–16 px`, weight `700`, uppercase with `1–3 px` tracking, Action blue.
- Hero metric: `52–86 px`, weight `800–900`, using Ink or the relevant entity color.
- Card title: `19–24 px`, weight `700–800`.
- Body copy: `16–19 px`, weight `400–500`, line-height `1.45–1.6`.
- Chart title: `18–22 px`, weight `700–800`; legends and axis labels `13–16 px`.
- Sources, footers, and page numbers: `11–13 px`, weight `400–600`, Body gray.
- Use uppercase for labels and short status pills only. Use sentence case for explanatory copy.

## Layout and components

- Cards use White or Paper backgrounds, `1 px` Grid gray borders, `12–18 px` corner radius, and a restrained shadow such as `0 4px 14px rgba(39,50,70,0.08)`.
- Comparison cards use a `4–6 px` top rail or left rail in the relevant entity color. Keep the two cards geometrically identical.
- KPI cards show one dominant value, one short descriptor, and one supporting comparison or status. Do not put more than three KPIs in a single card.
- Status pills use Soft blue, Soft teal, Soft violet, or a pale coral background with dark text; never use saturated fills behind long copy.
- Insight panels use Report navy with white text, an Action blue rail, and one explicit conclusion.
- Timelines use a thin vertical connector, circular milestone markers, a date aligned at the far edge, and no more than five milestones per page.
- Icons should be simple line or solid icons in the relevant accent color, placed in `40–56 px` rounded-square containers.

## Charts and data

- Use grouped bars for side-by-side comparisons, line charts for historical trajectories, stacked bars or donuts for composition, and horizontal bars for adoption or ranking.
- Action blue is the primary series; Signal teal is the second series; Insight violet is the third; Alert coral marks a risk or decline.
- Keep chart frames white with light borders. Use `#E3E8EF` for grid lines, a low-contrast axis, and direct labels for the most important values.
- Every chart must state its unit, period, estimate marker when relevant, and source.
- Add a short interpretation rail or conclusion card beside the chart. The chart should answer one analytical question rather than display every available metric.
- Use direct annotations for peaks, turning points, baselines, and forecast boundaries. Do not make the reader hunt through a legend to find the headline result.

## Page variations

- Cover: Report navy or deep navy background, a bold comparison or thesis title, a small series label, a restrained date block, and one short subtitle.
- Executive summary: a dark header, three equal KPI cards across the top, a large comparison chart below, and a dark Future Outlook or implication panel to the side.
- Economic scale: two equal entity cards with hero values, a relative-scale bar, and three short implication cards beneath.
- Growth trajectory: two stacked entity summary cards beside one large multi-year line chart with a clear zero baseline.
- Trade or timeline: a vertical milestone timeline on the left, a large trend chart on the right, and a small allocation chart plus dark conclusion panel below.
- Demographics or composition: two equal entity cards with matching donut charts, a shared legend, and one comparable dependency or composition metric per side.

## Imagery and icons

- Prefer maps, flags, products, locations, or diagrams that make the comparison concrete. Use them as evidence or labels, not as decorative wallpaper.
- Keep image treatment bright and inspectable on light cards; do not add heavy blur or dark overlays to evidence images.
- Use one consistent icon family and map icon color to the corresponding data series or status.

## Avoid

- Do not use dashboard density on every page, oversized decorative circles, glossy effects, or 3D charts.
- Do not mix dark body backgrounds and light body backgrounds without a clear role for each page variant.
- Do not use saturated red for ordinary comparisons; reserve it for risk, decline, or negative movement.
- Do not allow cards to become paragraphs. Keep each card to one message, one value, and one implication.
- Do not omit source notes, units, time windows, or forecast labels from analytical visuals.
